mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 55% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals abrupt labor market deterioration that triggers immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.4pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand recovery, driving sustained positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce a positive 60-day return direction in at least 65% of historical occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle indicates robust supply-side capacity that outpaces inflationary pressures, supporting positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth during tightening periods yields negative 20-day returns in over 55% of cases, or the signal loses predictive power when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in real personal consumption expenditures below -0.5% signals immediate consumer demand contraction that forces earnings downgrades and negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns remain positive in more than 60% of months where RSAFS drops below the -0.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising core inflation combined with a stable federal funds rate forces markets to price in future aggressive tightening, compressing valuation multiples and generating negative quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising CPIAUCSL and flat FEDFUNDS produces positive 60-day returns in over 55% of historical instances, invalidating the tightening expectation channel.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth exceeding 200,000 during a post-shock recovery phase confirms economic stabilization, reducing recession risk premiums and driving positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth above 200,000 fails to predict positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of recovery-phase months, or the signal reverses direction during expansion peaks.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month declines in the index of industrial production reflect immediate supply-side weakness that triggers short-term risk aversion and negative equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO changes coincide with positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in over 60% of observations, or the Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling windows.
